Advanced Cornea Care and Precision Laser Correction

Personalized cornea care and cutting-edge laser correction for optimal vision and safety.

Cornea Care for Corrective Eye Surgery

Corneal infections pose the major threat to the health of cornea in our environment. It is the leading cause of damage to cornea, resulting in corneal blindness. Many microorganisms can cause corneal ulcer, of which main agents are the bacteria and fungi. Virus is also well known to cause corneal infections. In corneal infection, there will be pain, reduction in vision and watering or discharge from the eye. Treatment is mainly with medication in the form of eye drops. Some cases that do not heal with medicines may require surgery – corneal transplantation for proper healing. Injuries are very common and they can result from minor to very serious damage to cornea, it is important to identify the extent of injury and treat them immediately. The outcome of corneal injury improves with early detection and treatment.

Treatment for Refractive Errors

LASIK

LASIK is a widely used refractive surgery that reduces dependence on glasses or contact lenses by reshaping the cornea with a laser. This procedure corrects vision issues such as nearsightedness, farsightedness, and astigmatism, offering quick recovery and minimal discomfort.

Eye Glasses or Contact Lenses

Eye glasses and contact lenses are common solutions for correcting refractive errors. They refocus light rays onto the retina, addressing imperfections in the eye’s shape, and improving vision for conditions like nearsightedness, farsightedness, and astigmatism.

Advanced LASIK Procedures at Giridhar Eye Institute

WaveLight Plus Innovision

An advanced AI-powered LASIK technology that creates a detailed 3D “EyeVatar” of the entire eye to deliver highly personalized vision correction. By addressing unique eye imperfections beyond just the cornea, it offers sharper-than-standard vision, reduced glare, faster recovery, and both flap and flapless treatment options—surpassing conventional LASIK.

  • Improved quality of vision with reduced glare and halos

  • Faster procedure time, completed in seconds.

  • Cold laser technology ensures better comfort during the procedure.

  • Measures the complete optical system, not just basic power

Wavelight EX500 Excimer Laser

Wavelight EX500 from Germany is US FDA approved and is one of the world’s fastest excimer laser technologies. This machine is known for precision, accuracy, predictability and is trusted by surgeons all over the world to deliver consistent results. This laser is tissue friendly also, as it ablates minimal corneal tissue per diopter of eye power. By “mapping” the unique shape of your eyes, the Wavelight EX-500 Laser allows the surgeons to perform an individualized laser vision correction just for you, resulting in a vision that is as clear as crisp as possible.

  • Smooth Treatment and High Comfort

  • Excellent Accuracy

  • Proven Safety

  • Multiple treatment options

Contoura Vision

In pursuit of picture-perfect vision, Giridhar Eye Institute now offers, for the ﬁrst time in Kerala,procedures assisted by the most advanced FDA-approved Topography Guided LASIK Contoura Vision technology. We now correct vision to a scale of perfection that is unmatched by any other LASIK procedure! It’s personalised LASIK, tailored to redeﬁne the quality of your vision!

  • Best vision outcomes, sometimes exceeding 20/20

  • LASIK customisation using 22,000 data points

  • Best results among all FDA-approved LASIK studies

  • Minimal injury to the eye and faster recovery

FEMTO-LASIK

Femto-LASIK is an advanced, bladeless LASIK procedure that uses a highly precise laser to create the corneal flap, providing a safer and more accurate treatment. This method enhances overall safety, ensures predictable outcomes, and allows for customized flap dimensions.

  • Completely bladeless for enhanced safety

  • Precise and predictable results

  • Customizable flap shape and size

  • Reduced risk of complications

Frequently Asked Questions

Explore common questions about Cornea & Laser Correction, offering detailed insights on procedures, recovery, risks, and long-term results.

How long does recovery take after laser vision correction?

Recovery is usually quick, with most patients resuming normal activities within 1-2 days. Full visual stabilization can take a few weeks.

Am I a good candidate for LASIK surgery?

Ideal candidates are adults with stable vision, healthy corneas, and no significant eye diseases such as glaucoma or cataracts.

What are the risks of laser vision correction?

Though rare, risks include dry eyes, halos around lights, or under/over-correction, but most are temporary and treatable.

Can laser correction treat astigmatism?

Yes, LASIK and other laser procedures effectively treat astigmatism, improving visual clarity by reshaping the cornea.
How long do the results of laser correction last?
Results are generally permanent, but natural aging may require reading glasses or further correction later in life.
What is the difference between LASIK and Contoura Vision?
Contoura Vision is a more advanced form of LASIK, offering enhanced precision and custom treatment for improved outcomes.
Is corneal thickness important for laser surgery?
Yes, corneal thickness determines your eligibility for LASIK. Thin corneas may require alternative procedures like PRK or SMILE.
Will I need glasses after laser correction?
Most patients achieve clear vision without glasses, though reading glasses might still be necessary after the age of 40 due to presbyopia.
